name: Dr. Shawn Wilson
subject: Weekly Update: Meeting Remarkable Louisianans
body:
image of ... [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/swlogo2.png]
TEAM WILSON WEEKLY UPDATES
Peter,
What a week it has been! I have had the privilege of meeting remarkable Louisianans, listening to their stories, and understanding their concerns and aspirations. Each interaction has reminded me of the strength and diversity within our state.
Educators Deserve a Permanent Pay Raise
In light of the legislature’s disappointing decision to give educators a one time stipend and deny them of a much-needed pay raise, I wholeheartedly commit to ensuring teachers are put on the path to receiving the funding they deserve, by including future pay raises in the MFP. My wife is a teacher, so I know how hard they work. Prioritizing our educators will also help to address the teacher shortage crisis Louisiana currently faces.
RE: Trump's Latest Indictment
The Advocate asked each of the 2023 Gubernatorial candidates for their thoughts on the indictment of former President Donald Trump. Here’s my reaction: [https://www.theadvocate.com/baton_rouge/news/politics/louisiana-gubernatorial-candidates-react-to-trump-indictment/article_9210bbeb-129e-5e85-8115-f7fdebc38491.html]
“An independent grand jury felt that the evidence presented to them was serious enough to warrant an indictment of 37 counts, and given the information that's publicly available, it is concerning. However, every American is innocent until proven guilty. Any breach of national security, regardless of the individual, should be prosecuted. President Trump will face a jury of his peers to determine his guilt or innocence. He's afforded that right as a citizen, and regardless of our political beliefs, we shouldn't jump to conclusions. Americans do not need distractions from the important issues facing this country, but his decision to run is his alone, and the voters will have the opportunity to weigh in at the ballot box.”
HIGHLIGHTS FROM THE CAMPAIGN TRAIL
Alexandria
ICYMI: We stopped in Alexandria, [https://www.kalb.com/video/2023/06/15/democratic-gov-candidate-shawn-wilson-stops-cenla/] where I got the chance to talk to the CenLa Democratic Women’s Organization about the most pressing issues facing Central Louisianans.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/alexandria.jpeg]
Colfax
We made our way to Grant Parish for a meet and greet with elected officials and voters in Colfax. Police, Jury & Council members, the Sheriff, Police Chief, State Representative, and residents were on hand and excited to visit.
We also visited the newest memorial, dedicated to the Colfax massacre. I want to express my deepest empathy for the victims and their families for the pain caused by this devastating tragedy. We were joined by the mayor, a descendant of the principle victim, and a descendant of an assailant, to bring about truth and understanding.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/colfax.png]
Shreveport
I was honored to be the keynote speaker for the Shreveport-Bossier African American Chamber of Commerce. It was a truly special evening.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/shreveport.png]
Howard University
I had the opportunity to speak to Howard University’s Louisiana Club. I thoroughly enjoyed the insightful questions from both students and alumni.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/howard2.png]
Kentwood
I had the privilege of attending Pastor S.C. Dixon's incredible anniversary sermon for Pastor Lee Allen Pounds, Jr. at the Sweet Home Missionary Baptist Church in Kentwood. It was truly a pleasure meeting Mayor Irma Gordon as well!
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/kentwood.png]
Lafayette
I headed to Lafayette to spend the morning with the One Acadiana Executive Committee. While there, I had the chance to discuss topics impacting everyday Louisianians.
Our discussion covered streamlining local decision-making for the Industrial Tax Exemption Program, crime prevention, infrastructure needs, and workforce development. These are the areas that desperately need meaningful collaboration to bring Louisiana forward. I plan to prioritize these areas if elected Governor.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/lafayette2.jpg]
St. Francisville
I am thrilled to report we had a terrific turnout at our West Feliciana Event. The Saint Francisville art community is truly a jewel! Small towns like this are the lifeblood of Louisiana, representing the backbone of our culture and our economy, where hardworking individuals and tight-knit communities thrive.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/st%20francisville2.png]
Angola
I visited Angola Penitentiary with criminal justice leaders, including retired Judge Arthuer Hunter Jr., learning about a nationally recognized re-entry program that reduces recidivism of formerly incarcerated people by more than half of the 45% rate of standard inmates.
They learn a trade/vocation, test in the prison, and are taught soft skills that can make six figure salaries a reality. FIPs come out as better citizens for their families and our state’s economy. This is a smart approach that is producing results and should be replicated.
As Governor, I will be smart on crime. I will work to increase and support law enforcement, embrace technology, work with District Attorneys, Judges, and other key stakeholders to help protect people and property, while holding offenders accountable. We can’t afford to not get this right.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/angola.png]
New Orleans
Earlier this week, I spent the morning with Michalopoulos, a New Orleans Artist. Together we toured an artist community of studios he has spent time developing, along with his personal studio.
Michalopoulos has an amazing vision for the long-term cultivation of the arts in New Orleans. I hope to empower this vision from the Governor's Mansion.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/nola.png]
National Federation of Democratic Women Voters
I also visited with the National Federation of Democratic Women Voters. I was honored to be on hand to welcome them to New Orleans with Louisiana’s Chapter President Gwen Collins-Greenup, who is also running for Secretary of State.
These women from all across the country are doing their part and then some to make a difference for our families.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/national.png]
UPCOMING EVENTS
Join #TeamWilson at the Freedom Fund Banquet by the Louisiana NAACP on Saturday, July 24th from 7-10 PM at Xavier University’s University Center Grand. Get tickets here. [https://www.eventbrite.com/e/naacp-19th-annual-freedom-fund-banquet-fayette-county-branch-tickets-27434338846?aff=oddtdtcreator]
https://www.eventbrite.com/e/naacp-19th-annual-freedom-fund-banquet-fayette-county-branch-tickets-27434338846?aff=oddtdtcreator [https://www.eventbrite.com/e/naacp-19th-annual-freedom-fund-banquet-fayette-county-branch-tickets-27434338846?aff=oddtdtcreator]
The Northshore Democratic Women’s Club will host Dustin Granger, candidate for Treasurer, and I for a candidate forum on Sunday, June 25 from 3-5 PM at Abita Springs Town Hall.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP50/1/104399/images/dustin%20and%20wilson.png]
To learn more about my candidacy visit wilsonforla.com [wilsonforla.com] .
With hope,
Shawn Wilson
HOST SHAWN AT AN EVENT [events@wilsonforla.com?subject=Host%20Shawn%20At%20An%20Event]
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/sw-web]
ENDORSE [https://www.wilsonforla.com/endorse]
https://www.facebook.com/ [https://www.facebook.com/] https://twitter.com/ [https://twitter.com/] https://instagram.com/ [https://instagram.com/] https://www.linkedin.com/ [https://www.linkedin.com/]

Wilson for Louisiana
P.O. Box 4572
Baton Rouge, LA 70821
United States

name: CaucusRoom
subject: ☀️ CaucusRoom Weekend Edition
body:
A Shocking Discovery No images? [Click here](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-e-aokjyk-trhiujldw-yh/) [](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aokjyk-trhiujldw-r/)
### ** Welcome to the Weekend Wrap Up! **

### ** Here are the top articles that CaucusRoom members shared:**
* Biden's in hot water over his [**Pride celebration**.](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aokjyk-trhiujldw-y/)
* What aren't they telling us **[about Taiwan](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aokjyk-trhiujldw-j/)**?
* Tucker responds to **[Cease and Desist](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aokjyk-trhiujldw-t/)** from Fox News.
* Dems want to use the **[14th Amendment](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aokjyk-trhiujldw-i/)** on Trump.
* Whistleblower has **[audio recordings](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aokjyk-trhiujldw-d/)** of Biden.
* More **[government waste](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aokjyk-trhiujldw-h/)!**
* Press Secretary**[violated the Hatch Act](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aokjyk-trhiujldw-k/). **And**[won't stop](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aokjyk-trhiujldw-u/) **doing it.
* The House passed a bill to**[save gas stoves](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aokjyk-trhiujldw-o/).**
* **[Migrants with ties to the Chinese Military](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aokjyk-trhiujldw-x/) **have been released in the US.
* The left is **[erasing women](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aokjyk-trhiujldw-m/)**.
* Too many **[squishy Republicans](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aokjyk-trhiujldw-c/)**.
* Biden has **[BIG plans](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aokjyk-trhiujldw-q/)** for the railroad. What an idiot!

### **Voters Are Noticing**
Online focus groups of swing voters say **[Biden is too old](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aokjyk-trhiujldw-a/)** and feeble to be President.
### **More for the Big Guy**
New bank records show the **[Biden's accepted as much as 30 million](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aokjyk-trhiujldw-f/)** from foreign nationals. **Shocking Discovery** **[Texas troopers busted a border smuggling operation](https://caucusroom.cmail20.com/t/i-l-aokjyk-trhiujldw-z/) **this week as they stopped a Mexican illegal immigrant who was smuggling a dozen illegal immigrants. | | Last week, Ron DeSantis praised Donald Trump’s three extreme right-wing appointments to the Supreme Court – but said that in his view, the “gold standard” of Supreme Court Justices are Sam Alito and Clarence Thomas.

That’s right – the same Sam Alito who **_authored the overturning of Roe v. Wade.**_ The same Clarence Thomas who said we should **_“revisit” the legality of gay marriage._** The same Clarence Thomas who regularly receives lavish gifts from **_mega-GOP donor and renowned Hitler enthusiast, Harlan Crow.**_ The same Clarence Thomas whose wife worked overtime to **_overturn the 2020 election._**

If Ron DeSantis or Donald Trump become president, they’ll appoint more extreme right-wing Supreme Court Justices like Clarence Thomas and Sam Alito – **_and we can’t allow that to EVER happen._**

clean_body: according to Ron DeSantis | | | Last week, Ron DeSantis praised Donald Trump’s three extreme right-wing appointments to the Supreme Court – but said that in his view, the “gold standard” of Supreme Court Justices are Sam Alito and Clarence Thomas. That’s right – the same Sam Alito who authored the overturning of Roe v. Wade. The same Clarence Thomas who said we should “revisit” the legality of gay marriage. The same Clarence Thomas who regularly receives lavish gifts from mega-GOP donor and renowned Hitler enthusiast, Harlan Crow. The same Clarence Thomas whose wife worked overtime to overturn the 2020 election. If Ron DeSantis or Donald Trump become president, they’ll appoint more extreme right-wing Supreme Court Justices like Clarence Thomas and Sam Alito – and we can’t allow that to EVER happen. =** | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = = = = = = Thank you for helping us to protect rights for Georgians and Americans across the country. – GA Dems | | Paid for by the Democratic Party of Georgia. With the help of you and this grassroots team, we made history last November – flipping four chambers, adding three new Democratic trifectas, and defending every single majority. **But we can’t take our foot off the gas now – the stakes are too high.**
State Democrats are facing a slew of special elections over the next two months – and the Virginia House and Senate are in play this November.
**Electing Democratic leaders who will support President Biden’s agenda in statehouses across the country is _critical_ to building a better future in your state. But it’s up to grassroots supporters like you to own a piece of this fight and give state Democrats the resources they need to go toe-to-toe against their Republican opponents. **
As an official member, you’ll be at the forefront of the fight to defeat extremist state Republicans, helping elect Democrats who will work to secure a progressive future in your state. **So can we count you in?**

name: PETITION (via Wexton for Congress)
subject: Congress must restore the Voting Rights Act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Wexton for Congress
body:
We cannot count on the courts to save us.

[ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/65001792/411963744/-772585877?refcode=EM_FR_230617VRA_N_PT_FULL_9_NG_N_Logo&fn=&ln=&em=dpwillis67@gmail.com&add1=&ci=Cresco&st=PA&pc=18326&oc=&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3Yzg5ZjU0OS02NzBkLWVlMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=he_qrQyN9y_C3IjB29h_S1lwcO2cQlxlNQQtSYJH3z0=)
---
Look, we're still breathing a big sigh of relief after the Supreme Court upheld Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act (which protects against racial gerrymandering) and struck down Alabama's unjust congressional lines.

But it never should have come down to a 5-4 decision. **Congress should have stepped up to strengthen the Voting Rights Act _immediately after_ the Supreme Court weakened the law in 2013.**

Jennifer is determined to use this moment to rally folks and call on Congress to do its part and legislatively protect the Voting Rights Act from further right-wing challenges.

[**Will you join her and add your name today? We're hoping to get 10,000 co-signers.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/65001793/411963751/1200666214?ms=EM_FR_230617VRA_N_PT_FULL_9_NG_N_L1&refcode=EM_FR_230617VRA_N_PT_FULL_9_NG_N_L1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3Yzg5ZjU0OS02NzBkLWVlMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=he_qrQyN9y_C3IjB29h_S1lwcO2cQlxlNQQtSYJH3z0=&emci=47d50f54-500d-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=7c89f549-670d-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=32654120) [Add Your Name ➞](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/65001794/411963752/1200666214?ms=EM_FR_230617VRA_N_PT_FULL_9_NG_N_B1&refcode=EM_FR_230617VRA_N_PT_FULL_9_NG_N_B1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3Yzg5ZjU0OS02NzBkLWVlMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=he_qrQyN9y_C3IjB29h_S1lwcO2cQlxlNQQtSYJH3z0=&emci=47d50f54-500d-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=7c89f549-670d-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=32654120) The Voting Rights Act of 1965 is one of the most essential pieces of legislation for our democracy. Its passage and impact is a testament to the blood, sweat, and tears of so many who dedicated their lives to justice.

But in recent years, Republicans have waged a sustained assault on the right to vote. We've seen voter ID laws passed by far too many states, purges of voter rolls, and cut-backs to early voting. **And the Supreme Court's disastrous 2013 _Shelby_ ruling opened the floodgates to much of this.**

So even though the court may have protected the Voting Rights Act this month, we cannot count on that happening in the future.

Jennifer has been unequivocal in her support of legislation to strengthen the Voting Rights Act. We need to have her back so that Kevin McCarthy can no longer ignore the will of the people.

[**Help us get 10,000 signatures to demand that Kevin McCarthy and Congress do what's right for the country and pass a renewed Voting Rights Act. Add your name here →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/65001795/411963753/1200666214?ms=EM_FR_230617VRA_N_PT_FULL_9_NG_N_L2&refcode=EM_FR_230617VRA_N_PT_FULL_9_NG_N_L2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MTA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3Yzg5ZjU0OS02NzBkLWVlMTEtOTA3Yy0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=he_qrQyN9y_C3IjB29h_S1lwcO2cQlxlNQQtSYJH3z0=&emci=47d50f54-500d-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&emdi=7c89f549-670d-ee11-907c-00224832eb73&ceid=32654120)

Thank you,

The team at Wexton for Congress | **Rep. Jennifer Wexton proudly serves the 10th District of Virginia.
